analysis_description: Round-3 tape mids for VEV_4000 and VEV_4500 vs VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT: Black–Scholes implied vol (no dividends, zero rate) and call delta from numerical IV inversion. TTE per calendar day index: historical file day k uses TTE = 7 − k days (7-day voucher clock; aligns with round3description example mapping day index to TTE). Spread stats from bid_price_1 / ask_price_1.
used_to_decide: VEV_4000/4500 show persistently wide top spreads (~21 and ~16 ticks) vs penny edge, supporting deep passive quotes. Mean BS call delta from mids ~0.994 on both strikes (deep ITM), so extract hedge skew is small but nonzero. Chose to anchor passive on 4000/4500 and use extract as delta ballast.

analysis_description: Small parameter grid on backtest: (v0) WIDE_SPREAD_MIN=18 vs (v1) 16; deep offset 7/5 vs symmetric 6; passive size 2 vs 3. Counted VEV_4500 rows in trades_round_3_day_*.csv: essentially no market prints for 4500 on days 0 and 2, one trade on day 1 — explains zero PnL contribution under worse-fill matching despite wide quotes.
used_to_decide: Lower spread threshold to 16 and size 3 improved merged Round-3 PnL (27938 vs 27014). VEV_4500 remains non-actionable in this historical tape for maker-style fills; thesis stays on wide-book passive on 4000 with 4500 quoted for completeness.
